judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all three expectations with essentially equivalent substance; Report 2 is somewhat more thorough (e.g., notes exports.py test-suite blind spot, active PHI-probing angle) but this does not change expectation coverage — the two are comparable.

report_1.evidence: backend/app/appointments.py, search_appointments() (branch lines 10–20)... The function accepts tenant_id but the SQL never filters on it. The query runs across the entire appointments table for all tenants

report_2.evidence: rosterly/backend/app/appointments.py, search_appointments, lines 10–20 ... There is no tenant_id predicate. tenant_id is accepted as the function's 2nd parameter... but it is used only as a selected column — never to filter

report_1.evidence: backend/app/db.py:7 — def build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id, filters): (the new filters param has no default) versus the un-updated caller backend/app/exports.py:9 — rows = build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id).all()... Every run of the nightly per-tenant CSV export will raise TypeError

report_2.evidence: rosterly/backend/app/db.py:7 (signature changed to build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id, filters) — filters has no default) versus the unchanged caller rosterly/backend/app/exports.py:9 (build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id) — 2 args)... Any call to nightly_export(session, tenant_id) raises TypeError

report_1.evidence: Raw f-string interpolation of status into SQL... this is an SQL-injection anti-pattern. It is not exploitable through the API today because AppointmentStatus is a fixed str, Enum (schemas.py) and FastAPI rejects any other value with 422, so status.value is constrained to booked/cancelled/completed.

report_2.evidence: Latent SQL injection (should-fix, not independently blocking): appointments.py:16 interpolates status.value into the SQL via an f-string... while q is correctly bound as :q. It is not exploitable through the endpoint today because status: AppointmentStatus is a str, Enum (schemas.py:5-8) that FastAPI validates to booked|cancelled|completed before the handler runs
